title: The Ever Given's Week of Global Chaos
subtitle: How a sideways ship in Egypt made your burrito more expensive

ripples.headline: Global shipping rates surge 47% as 400 ships queue in Mediterranean
Oil prices spike $4/barrel as tankers reroute around Cape of Good Hope
European toilet paper shortage as shipments delayed from Asia
German auto plants halt production due to missing semiconductor chips
UK coffee shops ration oat milk as plant-based alternatives stuck at sea
Small-town delivery driver works overtime as Amazon packages delayed
Denver burrito costs $2 more due to delayed avocado shipment

symphony_arc.opening: A container ship runs aground in the desert, wedging itself sideways across humanity's most vital shipping lane.
symphony_arc.development: The maritime traffic jam cascades through energy markets, manufacturing, and consumer goods as the global just-in-time economy reveals its fragility.
symphony_arc.climax: Entire industries grind to a halt while tugboats and excavators race against billions in lost trade.
symphony_arc.resolution: The ship breaks free, but the ripples continue for weeks, ultimately reaching your lunch order.

choices: How one ship paralyzed global commerce
The butterfly effect of maritime disasters
